title: henriettea granularis
intro: Henriettea granularis is a plant species native to South America, commonly known as "Guava of the Woods," due to its guava-like fruit and shrub-like appearance. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Henriettea granularis include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and provide antioxidant effects. Henriettea granularis is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in traditional cooking and as a natural dye.
